title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 2, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: The Alley, a new 6,500 sq ft food hall, opened at One Post Office Square in Downtown Boston.
Flann O'Brien's bar returns to its Mission Hill location, offering Irish-American comfort food and weekend brunch.
Ye Olde Pepper Candy Companie hosts a colonial candy popup at Old North Church through Halloween.

title: Mass. woman, 20, arrested after her Tesla hit 120 mph in Connecticut, police say
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A 20-year-old Massachusetts woman was arrested for driving a Tesla at 120 mph on I-395 in Connecticut.
The incident involved reckless driving and passing on the right in moderate traffic, according to Connecticut State Police.
The driver was released on $1,000 bond and is set to appear in court on July 7.

title: Fairhaven man charged with first-degree murder after fatal shooting, police chase
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Nicholas Bottella, 33, from Fairhaven, charged with murder after allegedly shooting a man in Mattapoisett.
The victim, 57-year-old James Rizza, was found with gunshot wounds and died in the hospital.
Bottella led a police chase through Marion and pleaded not guilty to 10 charges in Wareham District Court.

title: Brockton woman identified as Lakeville drowning victim
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Daisha Murphy, 25, from Brockton, drowned after falling off a jet ski on Long Pond, Lakeville.
The incident was reported around 5:45 p.m. Tuesday; Murphy was found at 12:40 a.m. Wednesday.
Authorities stated Murphy was not wearing a life jacket; an investigation is ongoing.

title: As teen sports betting rises, Mass. rolls out a new classroom lesson
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts launched a pilot program teaching high school students about the risks of online gambling and sports betting.
The program aims to educate students on gambling laws, risks, financial literacy, and identifying addiction warning signs.
The curriculum, reaching over 2,200 students, shows increased awareness about legal gambling age and reduces belief in gambling as easy money.

title: Jaylen Brown trade grades: Celtics get poor marks from multiple outlets for ‘baffling’ deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics trade Jaylen Brown to 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, inciting widespread criticism.
Media outlets award poor grades to Celtics for Jaylen Brown trade, citing questionable return and strategy.
76ers receive high marks, considered a title contender with new lineup featuring Jaylen Brown and Joel Embiid.

title: What led to the Celtics trading Jaylen Brown? Recapping a drama-filled offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
The Celtics trade follows a dramatic offseason, highlighted by Brown's Twitch comments and a 3-1 playoff series collapse.
Tension with media and rumors of frustration within the Celtics organization surrounded Brown before the trade.

title: 6 takeaways from the U.S. World Cup win over Bosnia-Herzegovina
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The US defeated Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 in San Francisco, advancing to the World Cup Round of 16.
Folarin Balogun scored before receiving a controversial red card, leaving the US with 10 players.
Malik Tillman's 82nd-minute free kick secured the win, the US's first World Cup knockout win since 2002.

title: Brighton man charged in 2025 killing of 15-year-old in Allston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Armani Collazos, 26, was arrested for the June 2025 shooting of 15-year-old Phillip Franco in Allston.
Collazos faces multiple charges including murder and assault with a firearm and is held without bail.
Miranda Bodden, 22, charged as accessory after the fact, was released on $2,000 bail. Both return to court August 8.

title: Bus driver strike disrupts bus service in Merrimack Valley
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit services halted due to a strike by Teamsters Local 170, affecting several Massachusetts towns.
The strike responds to a management proposal restructuring Saturday shift pay, impacting overtime rates and weekend work.
Negotiations include a 25% pay raise over three years; talks between the union and MeVa leadership continue.
